# LPLinkView

**Class**

A rich visual representation of a link.

## Declaration

```swift
class LPLinkView
```

## Overview

[doc://com.apple.Link-Presentation/documentation/LinkPresentation/LPLinkView] presents a link based on its available metadata. Use it to show a link’s title and icon, associated images, inline audio, video playback, and maps in a familiar and consistent style.

## Present a rich link

To present a rich link in your app, create an [doc://com.apple.Link-Presentation/documentation/LinkPresentation/LPLinkView], passing an [doc://com.apple.Link-Presentation/documentation/LinkPresentation/LPLinkMetadata] instance into its initializer. Then add the [doc://com.apple.Link-Presentation/documentation/LinkPresentation/LPLinkView] to your view.

For example, to present links in a table view, add an [doc://com.apple.Link-Presentation/documentation/LinkPresentation/LPLinkView] instance as a subview when populating each cell.

```swift
let linkView = LPLinkView(metadata: metadata)
cell.contentView.addSubview(linkView)
linkView.sizeToFit()
```

[doc://com.apple.Link-Presentation/documentation/LinkPresentation/LPLinkView] has an intrinsic size, but it also responds to [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/UIKit/UIView/sizeToFit()] to present a layout at any size.

## Creating a link view

- **init(metadata:)**: Initializes a link view with specified metadata.
- **init(url:)**: Initializes a placeholder link view without metadata for a given URL.

## Specifying metadata

- **metadata**: The metadata from which to generate a rich presentation.

## Inherits From

- NSView
- UIView

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
